name: Waratah Super Battery
notable: Australia's largest battery at 850 MW / 1,680 MWh. Core of the NSW System Integrity Protection Scheme (SIPS) enabling higher transmission utilisation after Eraring coal closure. Total AER-approved SIPS revenue $705.2M over 5 years (battery + paired generators + Transgrid network). Suffered catastrophic transformer failure Oct 2025 with estimated $50-80M revenue loss. Funded by Akaysha Energy (BlackRock) with $100M CEFC investment. Tesla Megapack 2XL.

timeline.title: NSW Minister directs Transgrid to carry out SIPS project
timeline.detail: Classified as Priority Transmission Infrastructure Project (PTIP) under the Electricity Infrastructure Investment (EII) Act. EnergyCo selected Akaysha Energy as SIPS battery service provider.

timeline.title: NSW Government announces Akaysha Energy as tender winner
timeline.detail: Akaysha Energy (backed by BlackRock) selected to build 850 MW / 1,680 MWh battery at Eraring site for SIPS services

timeline.title: AER issues SIPS battery service revenue determination
timeline.detail: AER determines the revenue Transgrid may recover for the SIPS battery service component. Contestable amount (battery + paired generators combined) is commercially confidential.

timeline.title: AER determines paired generation services revenue
timeline.detail: Revenue determination for paired generators that rapidly reduce output during SIPS contingency events. Third provider added May 2024.

timeline.title: AER approves Transgrid network augmentation revenue (~$105M over 5 years)
timeline.detail: Non-contestable component: ~$104.2M (nominal) for network augmentations (line raising, tightening, substation upgrades) and SIPS control and communications system. Revenue cap ~$106.8M for 2024-29 regulatory period.

sources.title: AER — Network Augmentation and SIPS Control System

timeline.title: AER publishes total WSB revenue: $705.2M nominal over 5 years
timeline.detail: Aggregated total revenue across all 3 components (SIPS battery, paired generators, network/control). Breakdown: ~$105M Transgrid network, ~$600M+ contestable (battery + paired generators — split redacted as commercially sensitive). Payments flow: NSW consumers → distributors → Scheme Financial Vehicle → Transgrid → service providers.

sources.title: AER — Waratah Super Battery Total Revenue
RenewEconomy — Giant Waratah Super Battery to receive more than $700m

timeline.title: Full commercial operations — 850 MW / 1,680 MWh
timeline.detail: Commissioned as Australia's largest battery and one of the largest in the world. Delivered ahead of original schedule.

timeline.title: Catastrophic transformer failure takes battery offline
timeline.detail: Major transformer failure resulted in extended outage. Estimated revenue loss $50-80M from SIPS availability payments. Significant media scrutiny of project reliability.

sources.title: RenewEconomy — Waratah Super Battery revenue haircut

timeline.title: Transgrid submits revenue adjustment proposal to AER
timeline.detail: Revenue adjustment due to commissioning delays and transformer failure. AER approved a reduction to SIPS battery service payments — amounts redacted as commercially sensitive.

revenue_breakdown.transgrid_network_and_control: ~$105M (non-contestable — line raising, substation upgrades, SIPS control system)
revenue_breakdown.battery_plus_paired_generators: ~$600M+ (contestable — split between Akaysha and paired generators is redacted as commercially confidential)

scheme_contracts.payment_structure: Quarterly availability payments from Scheme Financial Vehicle → Transgrid → Akaysha. Revenue reduced when battery unavailable.
scheme_contracts.funding_chain: NSW electricity consumers → distributors (Ausgrid, Endeavour, Essential) → Scheme Financial Vehicle → Transgrid → service providers
scheme_contracts.legislative_basis: Electricity Infrastructure Investment (EII) Act — Priority Transmission Infrastructure Project (PTIP)
scheme_contracts.sips_service_description: Battery must maintain guaranteed continuous active power capacity ≥700 MW and useable energy storage ≥1,400 MWh. Provides instant discharge when SIPS control system signals a network contingency, enabling transmission lines to run at higher capacity. Paired generators simultaneously ramp down to prevent overloads.
scheme_contracts.transparency_note: The AER has redacted the specific payment split between Akaysha (battery) and paired generators as commercially sensitive. Only the aggregated contestable total and the non-contestable Transgrid amount are public.

revenue_adjustments.outcome: AER approved reduction to SIPS battery service payments (amount redacted)

sources.title: AER — Waratah Super Battery Total Revenue
AER — Network Augmentation and SIPS Control System

cost_sources.description: Total AER-approved SIPS revenue (all 3 components: battery, paired generators, Transgrid network) over 5 years. NOT the construction cost — estimated total construction cost ~$1B (AFR). Battery share of the $705M is unknown (contestable split redacted).
CEFC investment in Waratah Super Battery construction

stakeholder_issues.detail: Major transformer failure caused extended outage, triggering SIPS availability payment reductions. Estimated revenue loss $50-80M. Significant media scrutiny from AFR, RenewEconomy, WattClarity. AER approved revenue adjustment March 2025.

sources.title: AER — Waratah Super Battery Total Revenue
